title: The 50 Best Posts By Pittsburgh Businesses
subtitle: Instagram posts the businesses themselves made, ranked by likes. The counterpart to the creator-coverage list.
angle: the businesses that showed up on their own feed this quarter. What they posted, what worked.
intro:
Fifty posts. Fourteen businesses. Eighty thousand likes. The takeaway from this quarter's rankings is less about volume and more about concentration: a small group of operators figured out how to actually show up on their own feeds, and the numbers did the rest.

The two businesses doing the heaviest lifting are Page's Dairy Mart in Arlington and Jeni's Splendid Ice Creams in Larimer, and both earned their spots the same way: specificity. Page's topped the entire list with 12,699 likes and nearly half a million plays on a single post about a pickle split sundae, vanilla ice cream with strawberries, chocolate syrup, and pineapple. It is a ridiculous combination, which is exactly why it worked. Jeni's, meanwhile, placed five posts in the top ten, including a 1 a.m. Gooey Butter Cake video that felt less like marketing and more like surveillance footage of a very relatable decision.

This list is the counterpart to our creator rankings, where outside voices are doing most of the storytelling about Pittsburgh businesses. These fourteen are the ones who showed up for themselves. In Issue 02, we'll look at whether that gap is closing.
